pattern: Because values fall in a tiny fixed range, prefix counts per value let each query test which values are present and scan adjacent ones for the smallest gap.
hints: The smallest absolute difference among distinct values comes from two values that are adjacent when all present values are sorted.
Values are bounded by a small constant, so precompute prefix counts of each possible value across nums.
For each query, use the prefix counts to list which values occur in the range, then take the minimum gap between consecutive present values, or -1 if fewer than two distinct values exist.

edge_inputs.description: range of all equal values returns -1
adjacent values give the smallest gap

stress.description: large array with many random subrange queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, size // 2)
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randrange(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randrange(l + 1, n)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [nums, queries]
